pi_items.source: Found in setup_task.sh, export_result.sh, and verifier.py as the required output path.
Found in export_result.sh and verifier.py as the required text output path.
Specified in setup_task.sh as the desired circular orbit altitude and verified in verifier.py as the target_altitude_km parameter.

The task instructions and setup_task.sh incorrectly claim that a True Anomaly (TA) separation of 14.84 degrees corresponds to ~100 km at an altitude of 450 km (SMA = 6821.14 km). A quick calculation reveals that 14.84 degrees corresponds to 1766.7 km of separation, meaning the author confused degrees and milliradians (14.84 mrad = 101.2 km).
Explicitly defined in the task description and setup_task.sh as '5 km trailing CHIEF'.
The verifier.py logic explicitly allows any final absolute separation <= 8.0 km to pass.
The verifier.py checks that the final altitude is within alt_tol (5.0 km) of the target altitude.
The verifier.py explicitly bounds both delta-V burns to be >= 1.0 m/s.
The verifier.py explicitly bounds both delta-V burns to be <= 100.0 m/s.
The verifier.py requires the reported phasing time to be >= 1.0 hours.
The verifier.py requires the reported phasing time to be <= 48.0 hours.

privileged_info_summary: Completed PI audit for gmat_env leo_rendezvous_phasing task by reviewing the generative synthetic task instructions in setup_task.sh and verifier.py. Discovered a major mathematical contradiction: the prompt explicitly instructs the agent to use a True Anomaly (TA) separation of 14.84 degrees, claiming this corresponds to ~100 km. In reality, at an SMA of 6821.14 km, a 14.84 degree separation equals 1766.7 km, revealing the task author confused degrees and milliradians (14.84 mrad = 101.2 km). The verifier parameters for tolerances (5.0 altitude, 8.0 separation) and DeltaV constraints (1.0 to 100.0) were all verified accurately against verifier.py.
